title: No replay attack protection in the authentication protocol
description: The password authentication during key exchange computes: clientPassword1 = SHA256(password || serverPublicKey). The serverPublicKey serves as a nonce, but since the server in acceptKeyExchange generates a new ECDH keypair for each connection (line 1218), there is no persistent server-side record of used nonces. If an attacker captures the key exchange and is able to make the server generate the same ECDH keypair (e.g., through a weak PRNG), the authentication exchange can be replayed. Additionally, the protocol lacks timestamps or sequence numbers in the authentication messages, so within the same ECDH session, message replay within the encrypted channel could be possible if the cipher mode does not provide replay protection (note: GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 modes do provide per-message uniqueness via nonce/counter, but the base AES-256-SHA256 mode may not).
recommendation: Add explicit nonce/timestamp validation to the authentication exchange. Include a server-generated random challenge in the key exchange that must be signed by the client. Ensure all cipher modes use monotonically increasing nonces/counters to prevent replay within a session.
